Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I should recall the standard P2P process flow Generally the steps are 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Issue Purchase Order 4 Receive Goods 5 Vendor Invoice Received 6 Match Invoice to PO 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now I need to check each case to see if the activities follow this sequence and if there are any out of order steps or missing steps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Starting with Case 1001 The order is Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Invoice Received Match Pay Close That looks standard No issues here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 The order is Create PR Issue PO Invoice Received Release Payment Approve PR Receive Goods Match Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait here the Purchase Order is issued before the PR is approved Thats a problem because you usually need approval before issuing the PO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also the invoice is received before goods are received which is odd because you cant match an invoice without receiving the goods first

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then they release payment before matching the invoice Thats definitely out of order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also the invoice is matched after the payment is released which doesnt make sense So multiple anomalies here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 The order is Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Release Payment Invoice Received Receive Goods Match Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Here payment is released before the invoice is received or goods are received Thats a major issue because you cant pay until you know the invoice is valid and goods are received

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also the invoice is received after the payment is released So the payment is made before the invoice is even processed Definitely anomalies here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 The order is Create PR Approve PR Receive Goods Issue PO Invoice Received Match Pay Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Receiving goods before issuing the PO is a problem You cant receive goods without a PO The PO should come before receiving goods

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So the sequence is off here Also the PO is issued after goods are received which is backwards Thats a big anomaly

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So summarizing the anomalies Case 1002 PR not approved before PO issued invoice received before goods payment before matching Case 1003 Payment released before invoice received and goods received Case 1004 Goods received before PO issued

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: These anomalies could lead to issues like paying for goods not received not having proper approvals or processing without proper documentation

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Its a risk for fraud errors in accounting and non compliance with procurement policies
